revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:比治山神社]] {{Unreferenced|date=2017-8-28}}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name={{ill|Hijiyama Shrine|ja|比治山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hijiyama Shrine|qq=}} | location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|qid=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|1=Hiroshima Prefecture}}{{ill|Hiroshima|en|Hiroshima|qid=Q34664|lt=Hiroshima|1=Hiroshima}}{{ill|Minami-ku, Hiroshima|ja|南区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-ku, Hiroshima|1=Southern District|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q411052}} Hijiyamacho 5-10 | latitude_deg=34|latitude_min=23|latitude_sec=18.3 | longitude_deg=132|longitude_min=28|longitude_sec=23.5 | deity={{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}[{{NDLDC|965812/35}} 『広島市史 社寺誌』]38 - 42頁（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）。2020th year 4月6日閲覧。
{{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名毘古神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸島比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
{{ill|Kurumazaki Shrine|lt=Kurumazaki Shrine|lt=Kuruma-yaki Shrine|ja|車折大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuruma-yaki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 車折神社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 車折神社}} | shakaku=Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}
{{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|qid=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} | established=Unknown | main_festival=4th Sunday in October | shinji= | honden=Three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | other_names= | image=File:比治山神社02.JPG | map=Japan Hiroshima | prefecture={{ill|Hijiyamachō|qid=Q24873288|1=Hijiyamachō}} | coordinates={{Coord|34.388416666666664|N|132.47319444444443|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Hijiyama Shrine''' （比治山神社） is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Minami-ku, Hiroshima|ja|南区|lt=Minami-ku|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-ku, Hiroshima|1=Southern District|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q411052}}, Hiroshima City, Hiroshima Prefecture. It was formerly a village shrine in the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Rankd shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, but is now a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|qid=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} listed by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|qid=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名毘古神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸島比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, and {{ill|Kurumazaki Shrine|lt=Kurumazaki Shrine|lt=Kuruma-yaki Shrine|ja|車折大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuruma-yaki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 車折神社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 車折神社}}. === Subordinate Shrines === Inari Shrine deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} == History == Originally, the shrine was located in a valley south of {{ill|Hijiyama|ja|比治山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hijiyama|1=Mount Hiji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11126130}} called "Kōban Valley" and was known as Kōban Daimyōjin or Kōban Valley. In {{ill|Shōhō 3|ja|正保3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|1646|en|1646|qid=Q6930|lt=1646|1=1646}}), it was relocated to the grounds of Shōraku-ji, a {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|qid=Q550182|lt=Shingon|1=Shingon Buddhism}} temple, and became its guardian shrine. During the {{ill|Kanbun era|ja|寛文年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbun era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|1661|en|1661|qid=Q7005|lt=1661|1=1661}} - {{ill|1673|en|1673|qid=Q7617|lt=1673|1=1673}}), it was relocated to its current location and was worshipped as the {{ill|Ubusunagami|en|Ubusunagami|qid=Q1392097|lt=Ubusunagami|1=Ubusunagami}} of nearby villages. In {{ill|1868|en|1868|qid=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 1), during the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|qid=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, the shrine's name was changed to "Hijiyama Shrine," and Shōraku-ji was abolished. The temple's head priest, Chitō, secularized and renamed himself Shikuma Arata and became the shrine's {{ill|Shinto priest|ja|社掌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto priest|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In {{ill|1872|en|1872|qid=Q7753|lt=Meiji 5|1=1872}}, the shrine was designated as a village shrine, and in {{ill|1907|en|1907|qid=Q2048|lt=Meiji 40|1=1907}}, it was designated as a shrine for offering {{ill|Kannushi|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. On August 6, {{ill|1945|en|1945|qid=Q5240|lt=Showa 20|1=1945}}, the shrine buildings were destroyed by the atomic bomb, and they were rebuilt in {{ill|1954|en|1954|qid=Q18581|lt=Showa 29|1=1954}}. In {{ill|1959|en|1959|qid=Q5302|lt=Showa 34|1=1959}}, it was listed as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|qid=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|qid=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. 比治山神社03.JPG|[[Beppyo Shrine]] == Shrine Priests == ; Chief Priest The Ōmi family, which serves as the chief priest of Hijiyama Shrine, was originally known as the "Ōnomi" family and came from the {{ill|Shake (social class)|en|Shake (social class)|qid=Q11588367|lt=Shrine family|1=Shake (social class)}} of the Fuchū Sōja in Aki County.[{{NDLDC|3010423/68}} 『広島史話伝説 第3輯（広島城下・周辺の巻、（続）己斐の巻）』]103頁（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）。2023rd year 11月25日閲覧。 The Ōnomi family is noted in historical records such as the {{ill|Intoku Taiheiki|ja|陰徳太平記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intoku Taiheiki|1=Intoku Taiheiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11658236}} for producing great warriors, but during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q117415390}}, they incurred the wrath of the Takeda family, the lords of {{ill|Ginzan Castle|ja|佐東銀山城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ginzan Castle|1=Ginzan Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11383214}}, and lost many of their ancient documents. The first generation, Ōnomi Chikazane, died in {{ill|1555|en|1555|qid=Q6579|lt=1555|1=1555}}.[{{NDLDC|9573579/163}} 『安芸府中町史 第3巻（年表編）』]大呑略系譜（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）。2023rd year 11月26日閲覧。 ; Shrine Priest The shrine priest, Shikuma Saburō, died in {{ill|1915|en|1915|qid=Q2084|lt=Taisho 3|1=1915}}, and Saburō succeeded him.[{{NDLDC|910378/125}} 『広島県紳士名鑑』]190頁（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）。2018th year 7月24日閲覧。 == Regular Events == * January 1 and onwards - {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (New Year's Prayers) * January 5 and onwards - New Year Company Safety Prayer festival * Second Monday of January - {{ill|Coming of Age Day|en|Coming of Age Day|qid=Q483226|lt=Coming of Age Day|1=Coming of Age Day}} festival * Day before {{ill|Lichun|en|Lichun|qid=Q1364685|lt=Risshun|1=Lichun}} - {{ill|Setsubun|en|Setsubun|qid=Q746798|lt=Setsubun|1=Setsubun}} festival * February 17 - {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|qid=Q118906710|lt=Toshigoi no Matsuri|1=Kinensai}} * April 1 and onwards - Upper Half Year Company Safety Prayer festival * June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|夏越祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Passage through the {{ill|Chinowa|ja|茅の輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinowa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=Ōharae-shiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11261910}}) * October 1 - Lower Half Year Company Safety Prayer festival * Fourth Saturday of October - Eve of the Autumn Grand festival (Held on the fourth Saturday and Sunday if weekdays) * Fourth Sunday of October - {{ill|Autumn Matsuri|ja|秋祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Matsuri|qq=}} (Autumn Grand festival) * Throughout November - {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|en|Shichi-Go-San|qid=Q1072387|lt=Shichi-Go-San|1=Shichi-Go-San}} festival * November - {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|qid=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} * December 31 - Grand Purification festival * First and Fifteenth of every month - Monthly festival * Fifth of every month - Matchmaking Prayers == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 『広島県紳士名鑑』国民教育普及社、1917th year 。 * 広島市編『広島市史 社寺誌』広島市、1922 - 1925th year 。 * 都築要『広島史話伝説 第3輯（広島城下・周辺の巻、（続）己斐の巻）』郷土史研究会、1969th year 。 * 府中町史編さん専門委員会編『安芸府中町史 第3巻（年表編）』府中町、1975th year 。 == External Links == * {{Official website|name=比治山神社}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|比治山神社|version=102011193|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:比治山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11547466}} [[Category:Architecture of Minami Ward, Hiroshima City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Hijiyama (Hiroshima City)]] [[Category:History of Minami Ward, Hiroshima City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]]
